filename: Responsibilities and instructions for lab manager.docx
relative_path: PERSONNEL/Lab Management team /Role descriptions /Responsibilities and instructions for lab manager.docx
full_text:
Anastasia Lundgren

24.01.2023

RESPONSIBILITIES OF A LAB MANAGER

ORDERS:

Orders: place an order and follow the orders’ status.

Orders need to be marked to Orders Excel file, also wbs from which the order was made. For reordering the same product you copy-paste the order info from Catalog numbers file. If placing a new product order – always add the product also to Catalog numbers file for future orders.

When you receive an order – mark the arrival date in the Orders file, you can also add location of the product, for researchers, if location is not standard. Check storage temperature! On the product box/vial write lab name and arrival date+your initials.

Antibodies – also add to Inventory file! Researchers need to know the exact location of the vial! Sometimes antibodies need to be aliquoted prior to freezing! Ask the researcher.

Bills: confirm the bill only when the actual product has arrived.

Ask PI about logics- which wbs to use for what purchases.

Remember to use RUP basic funds wbs and ONCOSYS money.

Check if the wbs is correct in SAP.

For credit card bills collect receipts, scan them, if needed. Paper receipts are stored in a separate folder.

PLASTICS, MQ-water, BUFFERS:

Plastics to autoclave: tips, eppendorfs -mark the type on the package, we have different: Sarstedt, Eppendorf Safe-lock, Eppendorf DNA-LoBind, 1.5ml, 2ml.

Sterile MQ water for cell culture incubators: Keep minimum of 4x1L bottles on the shelf. 800ml MQ per bottle can be autoclaved.

MQ water tanks refill when close to empty. Wash canisters 4 times a year. Mark the dates.

Sterile 1xPBS: make from PBS tablets, 1-2L at a time, then divide into 500 ml and 100ml bottles and autoclave.

1xPBS tank for Färkkilä lab – make new 10L PBS from 1L 10x PBS stock+9L MQ water. Mark dates. Wash canister 4 times a year.

CELL CULTURE ROOM:

Wash 2x cell incubators and 2x laminars minimum twice a year, or best - when Steri-Run reminder appears on the screen.

CO2 incubator calibration is done once a year, in June, before juhannus, by lab engineers, after you washed the incubators.

Follow CO2 level at least twice a week (Mon and Fri morning). Tell Karen to order two more CO2 bottles for ONCOSYS the same day you changed the last full one.

Water bath in cell culture room– is washed once a month, we have turns. Every other turn is our turn as it is done for two labs.

Cell culture wastes: usually taken once a week on Friday afternoon, obligatory if there are patient tissue wastes. Do not leave those over the weekend.

Change of the cell culture suction bottle – upon need, usually once in one-two weeks.

CHEMICAL REGISTRY and CHEMICAL WASTES:

Chemical wastes and Chemical registry: via Fortum, twice a year or even once a year is enough. When chemical is finished and if the container has chemical registry mark on it – do not trash the package, store it until it is retired from chemical registry! New chemicals (not buffers, not biologicals) need to be added to chemical registry.

FREEZERS:

REVCOS: scrape ice 3-4 times a year. Do not defrost! Before scraping call huolto to inform that you are doing long-term door opening, so that they do not come if they receive an alarm.

Dry ice reserve is refilled once in one-two weeks, box in the Vähärautio lab Revco. It is needed for IDS samples and for any sort of samples move. is in regular use!

Keep an eye on LN storage tank in Haartman.

You are a contact person in case of emergencies (freezers, etc.)

PATIENT SAMPLES COLLECTION AND KEEPING REGISTRIES:

Patient samples collection – if substituting a researcher. Keep patient data safe! Follow the Instructions file in ONCOSYS-Ova wiki. Mark all the relevant registries the same day! Samples table in ONCOSYS-Ova wiki, our G-drive samples table, Inventory (what was frozen: FF tissues, ascites, normal omentum) and Benchling (Benchling – as detailed notes as possible!, also copy sample photos there).

Inventories: keep up to date! When new equipment or reagent arrives always think where it should be added.

If aliquoting antibodies, inhibitors, growth factors, etc. – place tubes into correct boxes, mark on the map and in the master file. All the notes about reconstitution and aliquoting must be added to your notebook and on the datasheet (place datasheet into the folder kept in the lab) or into the reagents journal – notebook next to the datasheets folders in the lab. If there are mistakes in calculations or chemicals used this needs to be traceable! Aliquoting for most of the reagents is done in the laminar, even antibodies. Toxic reagents are aliquoted in the fume hood, laminar is not a fume hood!

SHIPMENTS:

Shipments. UPS primarily. Always check the country regulations! Outside EU shipments (Norway is already outside EU!) – requires Proforma invoice. Needs to be filled with extra care. Prepare dry ice in advance, especially if shipping on Monday.

RESERVATIONS and ARRANGEMENTS:

Booking seminar rooms for lab meetings and other occasions, marking in wiki and lab calendar lab events, creating Zoom links and adding them to Lab calendar.

Booking restaurants and activities for lab events – ONCOSYS PI money are usually used. Give the place our billing info via email prior the event. We prefer being billed than paying with a lab credit card – more work in SAP.

Welcoming new people to the lab – key application, grant accesses to G-drive, wiki, Google calendar, add to Slack, mailing list. Lab tour. Computer order. Computer order for students and researchers on grants- from Helpdesk computer pool - for free.

GENERAL CLEANLINESS:

General cleanliness in ONCOSYS – cardboard, paper, batteries wastes. On Monday morning – wash bowls after Friday’s Happy hour. Call maintenance if printer is malfunctioning. Bring more printer paper from 5th floor post room. Inform Karen if tea/coffee is finishing.

In general – keep common things on the same places, return after using.

Other people in the lab remember the locations.
